seoTitle: Change a Photo in a Canva Template
metaDescription: Learn how to quickly replace a photo in a Canva template while keeping your design consistent and on-brand.
intro: Swapping out a photo in a Canva template is one of the fastest ways to update your visual content for a new campaign or audience. This guide covers the steps to replace an image while keeping the rest of your design intact.

steps.heading: Navigate the Canva interface
steps.body: Open your chosen template in Canva and locate the photo element you want to replace.

steps.heading: Select your template
steps.body: Confirm the template layout supports easy photo swaps, which most standard Canva templates do.

steps.heading: Upload your photo
steps.body: Add your new image to Canva's uploads panel so it's ready to drag into the design.

steps.heading: Replace the existing image
steps.body: Drag your new photo directly onto the existing one to swap it while keeping the template's frame and layout intact.

faq.question: Will replacing a photo affect the template's layout?
faq.answer: No, dragging a new photo into an existing frame typically preserves the template's layout and cropping settings.

faq.question: Can I use Canva's AI tools to enhance the new photo?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva includes photo enhancement tools that can adjust quality, brightness, and other details after you swap the image.

faq.question: Do I need a Pro subscription to swap photos?
faq.answer: Basic photo replacement is available on Canva's free plan, though certain premium templates may require Canva Pro.

faq.question: Can I revert to the original photo after swapping?
faq.answer: If you haven't saved over the original design, you can undo the change or start from the original template again.
